title: Basic Muesli

overview.description: A simple homemade muesli made from rolled oats, dried fruit and seeds, ready to customise with seasonal fruit and yoghurt.

method: Combine all the ingredients in a large bowl.
Store in a cool, dry place.
Serve approximately 60g per person with milk or yoghurt.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional Swiss Bircher muesli was originally soaked overnight with grated apple rather than simply served with milk.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a balanced homemade breakfast mix that can be adapted throughout the year.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick breakfast made almost entirely from pantry staples, avoiding the added sugar found in many shop-bought mueslis.
chapman-tips: Use jumbo rolled oats for the best texture.
Keep dried fruit in fairly large pieces so it doesn't all sink to the bottom.
Store in an airtight container for up to a month.
Add fresh fruit when serving rather than storing it in the mix.
notes.batch-cooking: Make a month's supply at once.
notes.leftovers: Simply refill the storage jar as needed.
